zh.Zevenbergen, North Brabant, Netherlands: 荷蘭努-布拉班特斯文爾根
zh.lifeSummary: 當Willem II van Strijen於大約1215年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德斯特利恩時，他的父親Willem I van Strijen21歲，母親Christina van Lijnden21歲。他於大約1260年與Elisabeth van Arkel結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1285年3月27日在荷蘭努-布拉班特斯文爾根享年71歲。

zh-hans.Zevenbergen, North Brabant, Netherlands: 荷兰努-布拉班特斯文爾根
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Willem II van Strijen于大约1215年出生在荷兰-拉藍德斯特利恩时，他的父亲Willem I van Strijen21岁，母亲Christina van Lijnden21岁。他于大约1260年与Elisabeth van Arkel结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1285年3月27日在荷兰努-布拉班特斯文爾根享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Willem II van Strijen was born about 1215, in Strijen, South Holland, Netherlands, his father, Willem I van Strijen, was 21 and his mother, Christina van Lijnden, was 21. He married Elisabeth van Arkel about 1260. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 27 March 1285, in Zevenbergen, North Brabant, Netherlands, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Willem II van Strijen est né environ 1215, à Strijen, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Willem I van Strijen, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Christina van Lijnden, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Elisabeth van Arkel environ 1260. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 27 mars 1285, à Zevenbergen, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Willem II van Strijen um 1215 in Strijen, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Willem I van Strijen 21 und seine Mutter Christina van Lijnden 21. Er hat um 1260 Elisabeth van Arkel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 27. März 1285 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Zevenbergen, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Willem II van Strijen è nato il circa 1215 nel Strijen, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Willem I van Strijen, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Christina van Lijnden, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Elisabeth van Arkel circa 1260. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 27 marzo 1285, nel Zevenbergen, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.Strijen, South Holland, Netherlands: オランダズイド-hオランドストリジェン
ja.Zevenbergen, North Brabant, Netherlands: オランダノオルド-ブラバントゼベンバージェン
ja.lifeSummary: Willem II van Strijenが約1215年にオランダズイド-hオランドストリジェンで生まれた時，父Willem I van Strijenは21歳，母Christina van Lijndenは21歳であった。約1260年にElisabeth van Arkelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1285年3月27日にオランダノオルド-ブラバントゼベンバージェンで71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Willem II van Strijen(이)가 대략 1215년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 스트리젠에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Willem I van Strijen(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Christina van Lijnden(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1260년 Elisabeth van Arkel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1285년 3월 27일 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 즈에븐버겐에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.Zevenbergen, North Brabant, Netherlands: Zevenbergen, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Willem II van Strijen nasceu aproximadamente 1215, em Strijen, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Willem I van Strijen, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Christina van Lijnden, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Elisabeth van Arkel aproximadamente 1260. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 27 de março de 1285, em Zevenbergen, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Willem II van Strijen родился около 1215 г. в местности стриджен, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Willem I van Strijen было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Christina van Lijnden, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Elisabeth van Arkel около 1260 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 27 марта 1285 г. в местности зивенбиржен, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды в возрасте 71 год.

es.Zevenbergen, North Brabant, Netherlands: Zevenbergen, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Willem II van Strijen nació aproximadamente 1215, en Strijen, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Willem I van Strijen, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Christina van Lijnden, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Elisabeth van Arkel aproximadamente 1260. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 27 de marzo de 1285, en Zevenbergen, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, a la edad de 71 años.
